ddls:
CREATE TABLE "properties" (
  "property_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each property */,
  "borough" INTEGER /* Borough where the property is located */,
  "block" TEXT /* Block number of the property */,
  "lot" TEXT /* Lot number of the property */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the property */,
  "bbl" INTEGER /* Borough Block Lot number (BBL) */,
  "x_coord" TEXT /* X coordinate for geolocation */,
  "y_coord" TEXT /* Y coordinate for geolocation */,
  "edesig_num" TEXT /* Designation number for property categorization */,
  "property_type" TEXT /* Type of property (residential, commercial, etc.) */,
  "property_status" TEXT /* Status of the property (active, inactive, etc.) */,
  "zoning_code" TEXT /* Zoning code for the property */,
  "land_use_code" TEXT /* Land use code for the property */,
  "year_built" INTEGER /* Year the property was built */,
  "building_area_sqft" INTEGER /* Total building area in square feet */,
  "total_units" INTEGER /* Total number of units in the property */,
  "tax_map_number" TEXT /* Tax map number for the property */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("property_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_properties_borough FOREIGN KEY ("borough") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the borough and its characteristics */,
  "boundary_coordinates" TEXT /* Geometric boundary coordinates of the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each block */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough the block is in */,
  "block_number" TEXT /* Block number as designated by city planning */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the block, including notable features */,
  "boundary_coordinates" TEXT /* Geometric boundary coordinates of the block */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_blocks_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lots" (
  "lot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lot */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the block the lot is located in */,
  "lot_number" TEXT /* Lot number as designated by city planning */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the lot's features and uses */,
  "boundary_coordinates" TEXT /* Geometric boundary coordinates of the lot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lots_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES blocks ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "property_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the property */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the property was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken on the property (e.g., updated, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_property_access_logs_property_id FOREIGN KEY ("property_id") REFERENCES properties ("property_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "property_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical change */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the changed field */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the changed field */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_property_history_property_id FOREIGN KEY ("property_id") REFERENCES properties ("property_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
